items.question: What is the time complexity of `linked_list[i]` (access by index)?
items.explanation: You must follow i pointers from the head.

items.question: Which Python data structure is internally a doubly-linked list of memory blocks?
items.explanation: deque's O(1) at both ends comes from its doubly-linked block structure.

items.question: Which Python type combines a hash table with a doubly-linked list to maintain order?
items.explanation: OrderedDict's move_to_end and popitem(last=False) are both O(1) thanks to the linked list.

items.question: Why does an LRU cache use a doubly-linked list rather than a singly-linked one?
options: you need O(1) removal of any accessed node — its prev pointer makes that possible
Python lists don't support reverse iteration otherwise
items.answer: you need O(1) removal of any accessed node — its prev pointer makes that possible
items.explanation: Without `prev`, removing a node requires a linear search to find its predecessor.

items.question: Which technique detects a cycle in a singly-linked list in O(n) time and O(1) extra space?
options: store every visited node in a set
items.explanation: Two pointers, one moving twice as fast; they meet inside any cycle.
